step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("which anticonvulsants cause visual field defects")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

First state if you agree/disagree with prior reasoning, give one key point of support or counterpoint, and finalize with \boxed{{option letter}}.
Based on the retrieved evidence, I agree with the prior reasoning that vigilabatrin causes visual field contractions. Multiple sources confirm that vigabatrin induces bilateral concentric scotomas (visual field narrowing) due to its action as a GABA-transaminase inhibitor, affecting retinal photoreceptors and ganglion cells—a process that begins around 9–11 months post-initiation and progresses over time. This adverse effect is well-documented across pediatric and adult populations, often progressing to tunnel vision, and carries a persistent risk even after discontinuation. No other listed agents (levetiracetam, phenytoin, ethosuximide) are supported by evidence as causing this specific visual deficit.
\boxed{\text{C}}
